summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the Dead's sole Montana performance, celebrated as a historic standout show.
review: Fans rave that this recording delivers crystal‑clear soundboard quality and captures a once‑in‑a‑lifetime Montana concert, which many call one of the best shows ever. The incident of a thrown pitcher hitting Bob Weir adds quirky lore, but the music itself is praised without reservation.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews give 5‑star ratings, praising both the musical performance and the excellent recording quality; consistent high praise yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the recording as excellent with good sound; no technical flaws mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unique historic show with high energy; fans consider it one of the best Dead performances despite limited documentation.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir was struck by a thrown plastic pitcher during the encore, an incident noted by reviewers.
